theorem.statement: Under baseline-submodel slack, for every fixed beta > 0 the original dose-response class has minimax MSE at least c n^(-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)) for all sufficiently large n. This lower floor matches the published rho_n exponent when s >= d/4; when 0 < s < d/4, rho_n has a strictly smaller exponent and remains only an upper-side comparator, so no same-class upper endpoint is asserted.
theorem.justification: Bonvini-Kennedy themselves note that the treatment-density smoothness beta may enter their upper bound only through proof bookkeeping, so the full beta-frontier is the real unresolved mathematical content beyond the clean subregime.
theorem.gap: No published paper determines whether the beta dependence in continuous-dose HOIF rates is structural or an artifact of the current upper-bound argument.
theorem.consumer: Resolving this question would complete the anisotropic phase diagram and determine whether future adaptive confidence procedures must learn beta as a genuine hardness parameter.
theorem.proof_tex: Proof. Let C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), and let R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) be the minimax MSE for the causal dose-response value theta_P(t_0)=int_{[0,1]^d} mu_P(t_0,x)p_{X,P}(x) dx. The identifying equality with E_P[Y(t_0)] is the given consistency and ignorability identification statement; the present argument concerns estimation risk for this already identified functional. The quantitative certificate is lem:certified-beta-frontier-bracket. That lemma supplies the universal lower floor c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for every fixed beta>0. If s>=d/4, lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra identifies rho_n with n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}, so the certified lower floor lands on the same exponent as the published benchmark. If 0<s<d/4, lem:rho-deficient-regime-algebra identifies rho_n=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} and proves that its exponent is strictly smaller than 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1). Thus the unresolved content is now exactly what def:beta-frontier-handle says: whether the original class admits a same-class upper bound of order rho_n, an intermediate exponent, or a genuinely beta-sensitive phase. No proof below asserts a certified same-class upper endpoint.
